cases.title: Testing-plan §11 NFR-10 verification row — 100% committed attendance mutation audit completeness
preconditions: Testing-plan §5.1 audit persistence suite and §11 NFR verification matrix row for NFR-10 apply.
dispute-trail fixture provides lecturer manual correction, admin override, and close-time system absent assignments.
M08 Audit and Compliance module persists audit_logs with real database append-only constraints.
steps: Enumerate all committed AttendanceRecord create/update mutations in the fixture (manual correction, admin override, bulk correction, and system close assignments per BR-22).
For each mutation, query audit_logs by targetType AttendanceRecord, targetId, and occurredAt window.
Verify each audit row includes actorUserId (or system actor for BR-13 close), timestamp, target student and session, oldStatus, newStatus, reason where mandated, and correlationId.
Compare committed mutation count to audit row count for the same correlation scope.
cases.expected: Maps to testing-plan §11 NFR-10 minimum evidence: 100% mutation audit rows present for every committed attendance mutation with actor, timestamp, old/new values, and reason where required; zero mutation-audit gaps (NFR-10, CMP-01).

cases.title: Protected mutation path — M01 authorize gate and M08 audit write atomically with M05 ledger update
preconditions: Real database transaction spans M01 Identity and Access, M05 Attendance Ledger, and M08 Audit and Compliance modules.
Closed session exists with enrolled student AttendanceRecord status Absent.
Assigned Lecturer is authenticated, within manualEditWindow, and authorized for AttendanceRecord.update per M06 policy resolution.
Policy mandates reason for manual corrections.
steps: Capture beforeState: AttendanceRecord status Absent; count audit_logs rows for target studentUserId and classSessionId.
Invoke M05 correctAttendance through application service only after M01 authorize(AttendanceRecord.update) passes for Lecturer scope.
Query attendance_records and audit_logs tables within the same test transaction scope.
Verify M08 writeAuditEvent payload includes actorUserId, timestamp, target student/session, oldStatus Absent, newStatus Manual Present, reason, and correlationId.
Assert audit_logs row is append-only and immutable after commit.
cases.expected: Protected mutation path enforces authenticated authorized actor before ledger write; M08 writes exactly one immutable audit_logs row atomically with attendance mutation containing actor, timestamp, old/new status, reason, and correlation ID per NFR-10 and BR-22.

cases.title: M01 authorize rejects unauthorized mutation before attendance_records or audit_logs are written
preconditions: Integration database seeded with closed session and student AttendanceRecord status Absent.
Student actor is authenticated but AttendanceRecord.update is Deny per permissions matrix §3.2.
Application service invokes M01 authorize before any repository mutation.
steps: Invoke attendance correction use-case as Student with status Manual Present and reason.
Assert M01 authorize returns Deny before M05 repository update executes.
Query attendance_records for target student-session pair and confirm status unchanged.
Query audit_logs for new manual_update rows tied to the rejected request.
cases.expected: Unauthorized actor is blocked at M01 module boundary; zero attendance_records mutations and zero audit_logs rows are persisted for the denied request, preserving protected mutation path integrity (NFR-10, RBAC-01).

cases.title: SM-03 attendance status transitions via authorized correction produce auditable beforeState and afterState
preconditions: Integration database seeded with students in Absent, Present, and Late states on a Closed session.
Assigned Lecturer and AcademicAdmin actors are authenticated and within policy windows.
Audit query module can filter by targetId and actionType.
steps: Lecturer corrects Absent student to Manual Present with reason; capture beforeState, afterState, and audit row.
AcademicAdmin overrides Late student to Excused with reason; capture audit row.
Query audit_logs for both mutations and verify oldStatus/newStatus pairs match captured states.
Confirm audit rows include actor, timestamp, reason, and correlationId for each transition.
cases.expected: Each legal SM-03 AttendanceRecord transition via authorized roster edit or admin override commits with a queryable immutable audit record documenting beforeState, afterState, actor, reason, and timestamp per NFR-10.

cases.title: HTTP contract — authorized PATCH attendance correction returns 200 and writes queryable audit entry
preconditions: Lecturer is authenticated with valid Bearer token and assigned to target session section.
Closed session exists with student AttendanceRecord status Absent within manual edit window.
Policy requires reason for manual corrections.
steps: Send PATCH /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance/{studentUserId} with body { status: Manual Present, reason } and Idempotency-Key as Lecturer.
Validate HTTP status code 200 and JSON response envelope per API §2.3.
Query GET /v1/audit-logs?targetType=AttendanceRecord&targetId={studentUserId}&actionType=manual_update&from={mutationStart}&to={mutationEnd} as AcademicAdmin.
Verify audit entry includes actorUserId, timestamp, oldStatus, newStatus, reason, and correlationId.
cases.expected: PATCH returns HTTP 200 with standard { data, meta, error } envelope; committed mutation is queryable via GET /v1/audit-logs with immutable audit fields per NFR-10, FR-29, and BR-22.

cases.title: HTTP contract — unauthenticated PATCH attendance mutation returns 401 without ledger or audit writes
preconditions: Closed session exists with student AttendanceRecord status Absent.
No Authorization header is supplied on the request.
steps: Send PATCH /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance/{studentUserId} with body { status: Manual Present, reason } without Bearer token.
Validate HTTP status code and JSON response envelope per API §6.1 pipeline step 1.
Inspect data field and error.code value.
Query attendance_records and audit_logs to confirm no mutation or audit row was created.
cases.expected: Response is 401 with standard { data: null, meta, error } envelope; error.code is Unauthenticated; no attendance mutation or audit_logs row is persisted before authentication passes (NFR-10 protected path).

cases.title: Student denied PATCH attendance mutation — no attendance or audit side effects
preconditions: Student is authenticated with valid Bearer token.
Closed session exists with student AttendanceRecord status Absent for a session the student is enrolled in.
steps: Call PATCH /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance/{studentUserId} with status Manual Present and reason as Student.
Inspect HTTP 403 response, error.code, and absence of mutation summary in data.
Query attendance_records confirming status unchanged.
Query audit_logs for new manual_update rows tied to the denied request.
cases.expected: PATCH returns 403 Forbidden with Forbidden or OutOfScope error.code; AttendanceRecord.update is Deny for Student per permissions matrix §3.2; zero attendance mutation and zero audit_logs rows are written (NFR-10, RBAC-01).

cases.title: SystemAuditor denied attendance mutation — read-only role cannot trigger protected write path
preconditions: SystemAuditor is authenticated with read-only audit and attendance scope.
Closed session exists with student AttendanceRecord status Absent within a scoped section.
steps: Call PATCH /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance/{studentUserId} with status Manual Present and reason as SystemAuditor.
Inspect HTTP 403 response and error.code.
Query attendance_records and audit_logs confirming no mutation or audit side effects.
Verify SystemAuditor can still read audit logs via GET /v1/audit-logs within scoped read-only access.
cases.expected: Attendance mutation returns 403 Forbidden; SystemAuditor cannot mutate attendance records per PRM-05; no audit_logs row is created for the denied mutation attempt; read-only audit access remains available within scope.

cases.title: Lecturer denied out-of-scope PATCH attendance mutation without audit trail for rejected request
preconditions: Lecturer is authenticated and assigned only to section A.
Section B closed session exists with student AttendanceRecord status Absent; section B is not in lecturer assignment set.
steps: Call PATCH /v1/class-sessions/{sectionBSessionId}/attendance/{studentUserId} with status Manual Present and reason as Lecturer.
Inspect HTTP 403 response with OutOfScope or Forbidden semantics.
Query attendance_records for section B student confirming status unchanged.
Query audit_logs for manual_update rows tied to the rejected out-of-scope request.
cases.expected: Out-of-scope mutation is denied with 403; no attendance_records update and no audit_logs row for the rejected request; protected mutation path blocks unauthorized scope before ledger write (PRM-01, NFR-10).

cases.title: Rejected PATCH correction outside edit window writes no attendance mutation audit row
preconditions: Closed session exists with lecturer edit window expired per BR-15.
Assigned Lecturer is authenticated for the section.
Student AttendanceRecord status is Absent.
steps: Call PATCH /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance/{studentUserId} with status Manual Present and reason as Lecturer.
Validate HTTP 409 EditWindowExpired or policy escalation response per API §4.5.
Query attendance_records confirming status unchanged.
Query audit_logs and audit_logs table for new manual_update rows tied to the rejected request.
cases.expected: Rejected mutation returns 409 or escalation response without committing attendance change; zero audit_logs rows are written for the failed mutation attempt because no attendance mutation was committed (NFR-10 protected path, BR-22 applies to committed mutations only).

cases.title: PATCH attendance correction without required reason is rejected before mutation and audit write
preconditions: Lecturer is authenticated and within edit window for assigned section.
Policy mandates reason for manual corrections per BR-14.
Student AttendanceRecord status is Absent on a closed session.
steps: Call PATCH /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance/{studentUserId} with status Manual Present and empty or omitted reason field.
Validate HTTP 400 or 422 validation error with stable error.code per validation rules.
Query attendance_records confirming status unchanged.
Query audit_logs for new rows tied to the rejected request.
cases.expected: Validation failure returns 400/422 before protected mutation path commits; no attendance_records update and no audit_logs row; reason-required policy is enforced prior to ledger and audit writes (BR-14, NFR-10).

cases.title: Flow C — authorized manual correction mutation followed by audit verification via GET /v1/audit-logs
preconditions: Lecturer is authenticated and assigned to section with closed session.
Student AttendanceRecord status is Absent; lecturer is within edit window.
AcademicAdmin can query GET /v1/audit-logs.
steps: Lecturer executes PATCH /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance/{studentUserId} changing Absent to Manual Present with documented reason.
AcademicAdmin queries GET /v1/audit-logs filtered by classSessionId, targetId, and actionType manual_update.
Verify audit entry actorUserId matches lecturer, timestamp is near mutation time, oldStatus is Absent, newStatus is Manual Present, and reason matches request.
Attempt to identify any UPDATE or DELETE path on the returned audit entry and confirm immutability.
cases.expected: Critical attendance mutation produces immutable queryable audit record with actor, timestamp, old/new values, and reason; audit entry cannot be modified after commit, satisfying NFR-10 and AC-19 forensic review requirements.

cases.title: E2E-06 primary workflow — lecturer manual correction on protected path with auth, reason, and audit trail
preconditions: E2E-06 scenario from testing-plan §6.1 applies: lecturer assigned, session closed, student unresolved as Absent.
Lecturer is within manual edit window; policy requires reason.
Authorized role can query GET /v1/audit-logs.
steps: Authenticate as lecturer; navigate protected correction path via PATCH /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance/{studentUserId}.
Submit status Manual Present with required reason and Idempotency-Key.
Verify attendance_records reflects new status and checkInMethod Manual.
Query GET /v1/audit-logs filtered by classSessionId and studentUserId for manual_update action.
Confirm audit trail includes actor, timestamp, old/new status, and reason without bypassing authorization.
cases.expected: End-to-end E2E-06 manual fallback resolves attendance on protected mutation path: authenticated authorized lecturer commits correction with mandatory reason and full immutable audit trail per NFR-10 and AC-25.

cases.title: Lecturer browser journey — manual correction form gates reason field and confirms protected mutation with audit evidence
preconditions: Lecturer is logged in on desktop browser.
Closed session roster page displays student with Absent status within edit window.
Manual correction UI requires reason input per BR-14.
Audit log detail or correction confirmation surface is reachable for authorized lecturer.
steps: Navigate to session roster for assigned closed session.
Open manual correction dialog for Absent student; attempt submit without reason and observe validation gating.
Enter required reason and select Manual Present status; submit correction.
Observe success confirmation and updated roster badge or status column.
If audit or history panel is available, verify mutation shows actor, timestamp, old/new status, and reason.
cases.expected: UI enforces reason-required gating before submission; successful correction updates roster and surfaces audit evidence (actor, timestamp, old/new values, reason) without allowing unauthenticated or out-of-window mutation (NFR-10, AC-13).

cases.title: Student browser journey — manual correction and roster edit controls are inaccessible on protected mutation surfaces
preconditions: Student is logged in on mobile or desktop browser.
Student has attendance history including Absent status for a closed session.
Application shell may expose roster or attendance detail routes.
steps: Navigate to personal attendance history or session detail view.
Observe absence of manual correction buttons, status edit dropdowns, or reason forms.
Attempt direct URL navigation to lecturer roster edit or PATCH-equivalent UI route if exposed.
Verify access-denied state, redirect, or read-only presentation without mutation affordances.
cases.expected: Student cannot access manual attendance correction UI or trigger protected mutation path from browser; attendance status is read-only for Student role per permissions matrix §5.1 (NFR-10, AttendanceRecord.update Deny).
